endian.h(3) Library Functions Manual endian.h(3)

NAME

endian.h - 🔄 Network byte order conversion helpers

SYNOPSIS

Macros


#define HOST_TO_NET_U32(val)
#define NET_TO_HOST_U32(val)
#define HOST_TO_NET_U16(val)
#define NET_TO_HOST_U16(val)
#define CONVERT_ARRAY_HOST_TO_NET_U32(arr, count)
#define CONVERT_ARRAY_NET_TO_HOST_U32(arr, count)
#define HOST_TO_NET_U64(val)
#define NET_TO_HOST_U64(val)

Detailed Description

🔄 Network byte order conversion helpers

Provides convenient macros for converting between host and network byte order. Reduces repetitive htonl/ntohl calls and makes intent clearer.

Common Pattern: Throughout the protocol handlers, we convert numeric values between host and network byte order for transmission. This creates repetitive patterns like:

Bad (repetitive):

uint32_t width_net = htonl(img_width);
uint32_t height_net = htonl(img_height);
uint32_t channels_net = htonl(img_channels);

Good (using these helpers):

uint32_t width_net = HOST_TO_NET_U32(img_width);
uint32_t height_net = HOST_TO_NET_U32(img_height);
uint32_t channels_net = HOST_TO_NET_U32(img_channels);

Usage:

// Sending frame header
uint32_t width = frame->width;
uint32_t height = frame->height;
uint32_t size = frame->data_size;
frame_header_t hdr = {


    .width = HOST_TO_NET_U32(width),


    .height = HOST_TO_NET_U32(height),


    .data_size = HOST_TO_NET_U32(size),
};
// Receiving and unpacking
const frame_header_t *hdr = (const frame_header_t *)packet_data;
uint32_t width = NET_TO_HOST_U32(hdr->width);
uint32_t height = NET_TO_HOST_U32(hdr->height);
uint32_t size = NET_TO_HOST_U32(hdr->data_size);

Definition in file endian.h.

Macro Definition Documentation

#define CONVERT_ARRAY_HOST_TO_NET_U32( arr, count)

Value:

do {                                                                                                                 \


  for (size_t i = 0; i < (count); i++) {                                                                             \


    (arr)[i] = htonl((arr)[i]);                                                                                      \


  }                                                                                                                  \
} while (0)


Convert an array of 32-bit values from host to network byte order
    in-place. Modifies the array directly.




Parameters


arr Pointer to array of uint32_t values


count Number of elements to convert


Usage:




uint32_t data[10] = {...};
CONVERT_ARRAY_HOST_TO_NET_U32(data, 10);
// Now all values in 'data' are in network byte order



Definition at line 127 of file endian.h.


127 #define CONVERT_ARRAY_HOST_TO_NET_U32(arr, count)                                                                      \
128   do {                                                                                                                 \
129     for (size_t i = 0; i < (count); i++) {                                                                             \
130       (arr)[i] = htonl((arr)[i]);                                                                                      \
131     }                                                                                                                  \
132   } while (0)










#define
  CONVERT_ARRAY_NET_TO_HOST_U32( arr, count)


Value:


do {                                                                                                                 \


  for (size_t i = 0; i < (count); i++) {                                                                             \


    (arr)[i] = ntohl((arr)[i]);                                                                                      \


  }                                                                                                                  \
} while (0)




Convert an array of 32-bit values from network to host byte order
    in-place. Modifies the array directly.




Parameters


arr Pointer to array of uint32_t values


count Number of elements to convert


Usage:




uint32_t data[10];
memcpy(data, packet_data, sizeof(data));
CONVERT_ARRAY_NET_TO_HOST_U32(data, 10);
// Now all values in 'data' are in host byte order



Definition at line 149 of file endian.h.


149 #define CONVERT_ARRAY_NET_TO_HOST_U32(arr, count)                                                                      \
150   do {                                                                                                                 \
151     for (size_t i = 0; i < (count); i++) {                                                                             \
152       (arr)[i] = ntohl((arr)[i]);                                                                                      \
153     }                                                                                                                  \
154   } while (0)










#define
  HOST_TO_NET_U16( val)


Value:


htons((val))




Convert a 16-bit value from host byte order to network byte order.
    Typically used for port numbers and other 16-bit fields.




Parameters


val 16-bit unsigned integer in host byte
  order


Returns


Same value in network byte order (big-endian)


Usage:




uint16_t port = 27224;
addr.sin_port = HOST_TO_NET_U16(port);



Definition at line 96 of file endian.h.


Referenced by acip_send_audio_opus_batch(),
    av_send_audio_opus_batch(), client_crypto_handshake(),
    client_serialize_packet(), discovery_session_process(),
    packet_queue_enqueue(), packet_send(),
    packet_send_remote_log(), packet_send_via_transport(),
    send_crypto_parameters_packet(), send_packet_secure(),
    server_crypto_handshake(), and
    threaded_send_terminal_size_with_auto_detect().






#define
  HOST_TO_NET_U32( val)


Value:


htonl((val))




Convert a 32-bit value from host byte order to network byte order.
    Typically used when preparing data for transmission.




Parameters


val 32-bit unsigned integer in host byte
  order


Returns


Same value in network byte order (big-endian)


Usage:




uint32_t count = 42;
packet.count = HOST_TO_NET_U32(count);



Definition at line 66 of file endian.h.


Referenced by acip_send_ascii_frame(),
    acip_send_audio_opus_batch(), acip_send_error(),
    acip_send_image_frame(), acip_send_remote_log(),
    acip_send_stream_start(), acip_send_stream_stop(),
    add_webrtc_client(), av_send_audio_opus_batch(),
    broadcast_server_state_to_all_clients(),
    client_serialize_packet(), handle_image_frame_packet(),
    packet_queue_enqueue(), packet_send(),
    packet_send_error(), packet_send_remote_log(),
    packet_send_via_transport(), send_packet_secure(),
    send_server_state_to_client(), threaded_send_audio_opus(),
    threaded_send_client_join_packet(),
    threaded_send_stream_start_packet(), and
    threaded_send_terminal_size_with_auto_detect().






#define
  HOST_TO_NET_U64( val)


Value:


htonll((val))




Convert a 64-bit value from host byte order to network byte order.
    Typically used when preparing data for transmission.




Parameters


val 64-bit unsigned integer in host byte
  order


Returns


Same value in network byte order (big-endian)


Usage:




uint64_t magic = 0xA5C11C4A1;
packet.magic = HOST_TO_NET_U64(magic);



Definition at line 213 of file endian.h.


Referenced by acds_sign_session_create(),
    acds_sign_session_join(), acds_verify_session_create(),
    acds_verify_session_join(), client_serialize_packet(),
    packet_queue_enqueue(), packet_queue_free_packet(),
    packet_send(), packet_send_via_transport(), and
    send_packet_secure().






#define
  NET_TO_HOST_U16( val)


Value:


ntohs((val))




Convert a 16-bit value from network byte order to host byte order.
    Typically used for port numbers and other 16-bit fields.




Parameters


val 16-bit unsigned integer in network byte
  order


Returns


Same value in host byte order


Usage:




const addr_t *addr = ...;
uint16_t port = NET_TO_HOST_U16(addr->sin_port);



Definition at line 111 of file endian.h.


Referenced by acds_websocket_client_handler(),
    acip_client_receive_and_dispatch(),
    acip_server_receive_and_dispatch(), client_crypto_handshake(),
    client_dispatch_thread(), client_parse_packet(),
    client_receive_thread(), client_send_thread_func(),
    crypto_handshake_set_parameters(),
    discovery_session_process(), handle_audio_opus_batch_packet(),
    handle_client_capabilities_packet(),
    handle_protocol_version_packet(), handle_size_packet(),
    packet_decrypt_envelope(), packet_parse_remote_log(),
    packet_queue_try_dequeue(), packet_queue_validate_packet(),
    packet_receive_via_transport(), process_encrypted_packet(),
    receive_packet_secure(), server_connection_establish(),
    server_crypto_handshake(), tcp_client_connect(),
    webrtc_peer_manager_handle_ice(), and
    webrtc_peer_manager_handle_sdp().






#define
  NET_TO_HOST_U32( val)


Value:


ntohl((val))




Convert a 32-bit value from network byte order to host byte order.
    Typically used when receiving data from the network.




Parameters


val 32-bit unsigned integer in network byte
  order


Returns


Same value in host byte order


Usage:




const packet_t *pkt = (const packet_t *)data;
uint32_t count = NET_TO_HOST_U32(pkt->count);



Definition at line 81 of file endian.h.


Referenced by acds_websocket_client_handler(),
    acip_client_receive_and_dispatch(),
    acip_server_receive_and_dispatch(),
    audio_dequantize_samples(), client_dispatch_thread(),
    client_parse_packet(), client_receive_thread(),
    frame_extract_dimensions(), frame_extract_new_header(),
    handle_audio_batch_packet(), handle_audio_opus_packet(),
    handle_client_capabilities_packet(),
    handle_client_join_packet(), handle_image_frame_packet(),
    handle_stream_start_packet(), handle_stream_stop_packet(),
    packet_decrypt_envelope(), packet_parse_error_message(),
    packet_parse_opus_batch(), packet_parse_remote_log(),
    packet_queue_try_dequeue(), packet_queue_validate_packet(),
    packet_receive_via_transport(), process_encrypted_packet(),
    and receive_packet_secure().






#define
  NET_TO_HOST_U64( val)


Value:


ntohll((val))




Convert a 64-bit value from network byte order to host byte order.
    Typically used when receiving data from the network.




Parameters


val 64-bit unsigned integer in network byte
  order


Returns


Same value in host byte order


Usage:




const packet_t *pkt = (const packet_t *)data;
uint64_t magic = NET_TO_HOST_U64(pkt->magic);



Definition at line 228 of file endian.h.


Referenced by client_parse_packet(),
    packet_queue_free_packet(), packet_queue_try_dequeue(),
    packet_queue_validate_packet(), and
  receive_packet_secure().
